THE CASE IS HEREBY DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. COUNSEL KAITLYN MURPHY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH CONFORMS WITH THE RULING ISSUED FROM THE BENCH, WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication, Against Plaintiff Michael Phillips
Off Calendar Apr-04-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Michael Phillips For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery And Seeking Costs
Off Calendar Feb-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff Michael Phillips, in pro per present (tel: 415-505-4123).
Atty. Kaitlyn M. Murphy, Esq. (via Zoom videoconference) of the City Attorney's Office (tel: 415-554-6762) for Defendants.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS LONDON BREED, IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, CARMEN CHU's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Michael Phillips For Failure To Comply With Court Order Compelling Plaintiffs Responses To Discovery And Seeking Costs.

Matter discussed. Consel Kaitlyn Murphy states the case has not settled as to Plaintiff Michael Phillips. Plaintiff Michael Phillips states he no longer wishes to proceed with this litigation and requests the Court dismiss the case with prejudice.

The Court vacates its tentative ruling and grants Defendants' motion thereof. The case is hereby dismissed with prejudice.

Counsel Kaitlyn Murphy is to prepare and submit a proposed order which conforms with the ruling issued from the bench, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Phillips Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Deem Admitted Requests For Admission
Off Calendar Dec-12-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: DEFENDANTs LONDON BREED , IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS MAYOR OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS,and CARMEN CHU's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Phillips Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Deem Admitted Requests For Admission.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants London Breed (in her official capacity as Mayor of the City and County of San Francisco), Jose Cisneros (in his official capacity as Treasurer of the City and County of San Francisco) and Carmen Chu's (in her official capacity as City Administrator of the City and County of San Francisco) Motion To Compel Plaintiff Michael Phillips Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents, And To Deem Admitted Requests For Admission, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Good cause appearing and the statutory criteria for such orders having been met, the court orders: (1) Plaintiff Michael Phillips must serve verified answers, without objection, to Form Interrogatories (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, within twenty (20) days of service of notice of this order; (2) Plaintiff Michael Phillips must serve verified answers, without objection, to Special Interrogatories (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, within twenty (20) days of service of notice of this order; (3) Plaintiff Michael Phillips must serve verified answers, without objection, to Request for Production of Documents (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, within twenty (20) days of service of notice of this order; and, (4) Each request for admission to Plaintiff Michael Phillips made in the City's Request for Admission (Set Number One), served on Plaintiff via U.S. Mail on September 5, 2024, is deemed admitted.

Order Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. =(301/JMQ)
